evidence: Registry payload includes entrypoints, risk, side_effect, safe_previews, MCP mirrors, and JSON typo aliases.
Ranks work with reasons/commands/validations; adapter failures degrade to warnings, but live adapters can add latency.
Graph and algorithms are structured; this pass moved invalid algorithm JSON intent out of argparse text errors.
Explains nodes/commands with related specs; this pass added bare command aliases such as explain next.
Grouped ranked hits; this pass fixed the advertised safe-first example so it includes a query.

side_effect: none by default; local_write only with create --write
evidence: Replay/diff are structured; this pass added a structured payload for snap --format json with no action. Full flag-position tolerance remains deferred.
Registry advertises MCP mirror names and graph exposes command->tool edges; full unified error envelope remains deferred.
Machine-readable wrapper contract with skill verb map; subagent found robot-docs safe-first text/JSON drift.
Human workflow is useful; JSON intent and auto-fix failure aggregation need a dedicated follow-up bead.
Capabilities advertise safe preview and fix_command extraction; JSON argparse errors remain deferred.
JSON stdout is parseable, but read-side commands eagerly load operator secrets and warn on stderr in this checkout.
Dry-run up is structured; direct down confirmation and status write semantics need a dedicated follow-up bead.
MCP mutators are safer than direct CLI; operator_doctor lacks safe skip flags and status readOnlyHint does not expose cache writes.
